Control Module

Base Controller Module

Field-Oriented Control (FOC) Module

src.control.transforms

Coordinate Transformations for Motor Control :2: (WARNING/2) Title underline too short. Coordinate Transformations for Motor Control ===========================================

Base Controller Module

This module provides the abstract base class for all control blocks.

Defines the interface that all controllers must implement for consistency and future device integration (e.g., FOC, sensorless control).

author:

BLDC Control Team

version:

1.0.0

Added in version 1.0.0: Initial base controller implementation

class src.control.base_controller.BaseController[source]

Bases: ABC

Abstract base class for motor controllers.

All control algorithms (V/f, FOC, etc.) inherit from this class.

Minimum interface: - update(dt): Calculate control output - reset(): Reset to initial state - get_state(): Return current state

abstractmethod update(dt)[source]

Update controller and calculate control output.

Parameters:

dt (float) – Time step [seconds]

Returns:

Control output (controller-specific)

abstractmethod reset()[source]

Reset controller to initial state.

Return type:

None

abstractmethod get_state()[source]

Get controller state variables.

Returns:

Dictionary with controller state

Return type:

dict

Field-Oriented Control (FOC) Module

Implements basic FOC algorithm with PI current regulators and optional auto-tuning functionality. Supports choosing Clarke or Concordia transforms and running in either polar or Cartesian output modes.

author:

BLDC Control Team

version:

1.0.0

Added in version 1.0.0: Initial FOC implementation with PI regulators and auto-tune stub

class src.control.foc_controller.FOCController(motor, use_concordia=False, output_cartesian=False, enable_speed_loop=False)[source]

Bases: BaseController

Basic Field-Oriented Controller supporting current loops and speed reference. Designed for integration with SVM generator, returning either (magnitude, angle) or Cartesian (v_alpha, v_beta) depending on mode.

Features:

  • d/q current PI regulators

  • speed reference (via iq setpoint)

  • optional auto-tuning stub

  • transform choice (clarke or concordia)

  • output in polar or Cartesian

__init__(motor, use_concordia=False, output_cartesian=False, enable_speed_loop=False)[source]

Initialize FOC controller.

Parameters:

  • motor (BLDCMotor) – BLDCMotor instance for state feedback

  • use_concordia (bool) – Choose concordia transform instead of Clarke

  • output_cartesian (bool) – If True, return (v_alpha, v_beta) instead of magnitude/angle

set_angle_observer(mode='Measured')[source]

Set electrical angle observer mode: Measured, PLL or SMO.

Return type:

None

set_startup_transition(enabled, initial_mode='Measured', min_speed_rpm=300.0, min_elapsed_s=0.05, min_emf_v=0.5, min_confidence=0.6, confidence_hold_s=0.02, confidence_hysteresis=0.1, fallback_enabled=True, fallback_hold_s=0.03)[source]

Configure observer startup handoff from an initial to target mode.

Return type:

None

set_startup_sequence(enabled, align_duration_s=0.05, align_current_a=1.5, align_angle_deg=0.0, open_loop_initial_speed_rpm=30.0, open_loop_target_speed_rpm=300.0, open_loop_ramp_time_s=0.2, open_loop_id_ref_a=0.0, open_loop_iq_ref_a=2.0)[source]

Configure a standard sensored/sensorless startup sequence.

Return type:

None

set_pll_gains(kp, ki)[source]

Set PLL observer gains.

Return type:

None

set_smo_gains(k_slide, lpf_alpha, boundary=0.06)[source]

Set SMO-like observer gains and smoothing constants.

Return type:

None

set_sensorless_blend(enabled=True, min_speed_rpm=250.0, min_confidence=0.65)[source]

Configure low-speed sensorless angle blending behavior.

Return type:

None

set_cascaded_speed_loop(enabled, iq_limit_a=None)[source]

Enable/disable cascaded speed->iq loop and optionally set iq clamp.

Return type:

None

set_speed_pi_gains(kp, ki, kaw=0.3)[source]

Set speed loop PI gains for cascaded control mode.

Return type:

None

set_decoupling(enable_d=False, enable_q=False)[source]

Enable/disable optional d/q decoupling feed-forward compensation.

Return type:

None

set_current_pi_gains(d_kp, d_ki, q_kp, q_ki, kaw=0.5)[source]

Set d/q current PI gains and anti-windup gain.

Return type:

None

set_voltage_saturation(mode='proportional', coupled_antiwindup_enabled=False, coupled_antiwindup_gain=0.15)[source]

Configure dq voltage saturation strategy and optional coupled anti-windup.

Return type:

None

set_current_references(id_ref, iq_ref)[source]

Set d/q current references.

Return type:

None

set_speed_reference(speed_rpm)[source]

Set speed reference (rpm). qi reference will be derived from it.

Return type:

None

set_field_weakening(enabled, start_speed_rpm=0.0, gain=1.0, max_negative_id_a=0.0, headroom_target_v=None)[source]

Configure voltage-headroom-based field-weakening d-axis current injection.

Return type:

None

auto_tune_pi(axis='q', bandwidth=50.0)[source]

Auto-tune PI parameters for given axis (‘d’ or ‘q’).

This is a placeholder using simple heuristics. A real implementation would perform step responses to estimate Ku and Tu and apply Ziegler–Nichols or IMC rules.

Return type:

None

update(dt)[source]

Update controller and return voltage command.

Parameters:

dt (float) – Time step [s]

Return type:

Tuple[float, float]

Returns:

Either (magnitude, angle) or (v_alpha, v_beta) depending on output_cartesian setting.

reset()[source]

Reset controller state.

Return type:

None

get_state()[source]

Return controller state variables.

Return type:

dict

V/f Controller Module

This module implements a Voltage-to-Frequency (V/f) control block for BLDC motor.

V/f control provides a simple, effective way to control motor speed without complex feedback systems. It generates a voltage ramp profile proportional to desired frequency/speed.

Commonly used for: - Motor soft-start - Speed ramping - Simple open-loop speed control - Sequential voltage buildup

author:

BLDC Control Team

version:

1.0.0

Added in version 1.0.0: Initial V/f controller implementation

class src.control.vf_controller.VFController(v_nominal, f_nominal, dc_voltage=48.0, v_startup=1.0, ramp_rate=10.0)[source]

Bases: BaseController

Voltage-to-Frequency (V/f) Controller

Implements V/f characteristic for open-loop motor control.

V/f Relationship:

\[V(f) = V_0 + K_{vf} \cdot f\]
Where:

  • V₀: Starting voltage (to overcome friction)

  • K_vf: V/f slope constant

  • f: Desired frequency [Hz]

Features: - Linear V/f characteristic - Voltage ramp limiting (soft-start) - Frequency slew-rate limiting - Voltage saturation - Real-time modulation output

Example:
>>> controller = VFController(
...     v_nominal=48.0,
...     f_nominal=100.0,
...     dc_voltage=48.0
... )
>>> controller.set_speed_reference(50.0)  # Hz
>>>
>>> for i in range(100):
...     mag, angle = controller.update(dt=0.001)
...     # Apply to SVM
__init__(v_nominal, f_nominal, dc_voltage=48.0, v_startup=1.0, ramp_rate=10.0)[source]

Initialize V/f controller.

Parameters:

  • v_nominal (float) – Nominal motor voltage [V]

  • f_nominal (float) – Nominal motor frequency [Hz]

  • dc_voltage (float) – DC bus voltage [V], defaults to 48.0

  • v_startup (float) – Starting voltage to overcome friction [V], defaults to 1.0

  • ramp_rate (float) – Voltage ramp rate limit [V/s], defaults to 10.0

Raises:

  • ValueError – If f_nominal <= 0

  • ValueError – If v_nominal <= 0

set_startup_sequence(enable=True, align_duration_s=0.05, align_voltage_v=1.5, align_angle_deg=0.0, ramp_initial_frequency_hz=2.0)[source]

Configure the standard V/f startup sequence.

Return type:

None

set_speed_reference(frequency)[source]

Set reference frequency (speed).

Parameters:

frequency (float) – Desired frequency [Hz], can be negative for reverse

Return type:

None

get_speed_reference()[source]

Get current speed reference.

Returns:

Reference frequency [Hz]

Return type:

float

update(dt)[source]

Update controller state and generate voltage command.

Algorithm: 1. Apply frequency slew-rate limiting 2. Calculate V/f voltage from frequency 3. Apply startup boost if needed 4. Saturate voltage to maximum 5. Update phase angle 6. Return voltage magnitude and angle

Parameters:

dt (float) – Time step [seconds]

Returns:

Tuple of (magnitude [V], angle [rad])

Return type:

tuple

\[f_{actual}(t+dt) = f_{actual} + \text{clamp}(f_{ref} - f_{actual}, -\Delta f, \Delta f)\]
\[V(t) = V_0 + K_{vf} \cdot |f_{actual}|\]
\[\theta(t+dt) = \theta(t) + 2\pi \cdot f_{actual} \cdot dt\]
set_vf_characteristic(v_startup, v_nominal, f_nominal)[source]

Configure V/f characteristic.

Parameters:

  • v_startup (float) – Startup voltage [V]

  • v_nominal (float) – Nominal voltage [V]

  • f_nominal (float) – Nominal frequency [Hz]

Raises:

ValueError – If f_nominal <= 0

Return type:

None

set_frequency_slew_rate(slew_rate)[source]

Set frequency ramp rate limit.

Parameters:

slew_rate (float) – Maximum frequency change [Hz/s]

Raises:

ValueError – If slew_rate < 0

Return type:

None

set_voltage_ramp_rate(ramp_rate)[source]

Set voltage ramp rate limit.

Parameters:

ramp_rate (float) – Maximum voltage change [V/s]

Raises:

ValueError – If ramp_rate < 0

Return type:

None

enable_startup_boost(enable=True, duration=0.5)[source]

Enable/disable startup boost phase.

Startup boost applies extra voltage for specified duration to help break inertia and accelerate the motor faster at low speeds.

Parameters:

  • enable (bool) – Enable or disable boost

  • duration (float) – Boost duration [seconds]

Return type:

None

reset()[source]

Reset controller to initial state.

Return type:

None

get_state()[source]

Get controller state variables.

Returns:

State dictionary

Return type:

dict

Keys:

  • ‘frequency_ref’: Reference frequency [Hz]

  • ‘frequency_actual’: Actual frequency [Hz]

  • ‘voltage’: Actual voltage magnitude [V]

  • ‘angle’: Voltage phase angle [rad]

SVM Generator Module

This module implements Space Vector Modulation (SVM) for 3-phase voltage generation and includes an optional stateful inverter-realism layer.

The realism layer is deliberately lightweight compared to a switching-device transient simulator, but it captures the most important inverter effects used in drive-system studies:

  • Constant semiconductor conduction drop

  • Current-dependent conduction loss

  • Switching-frequency-dependent voltage loss

  • Direction-aware dead-time distortion

  • Freewheel diode path loss

  • Minimum pulse suppression near zero voltage

  • DC-link capacitor/ESR/source-resistance dynamics

  • Junction-temperature-dependent parameter drift

  • Per-phase asymmetry and mismatch

Each realism feature can be enabled or disabled independently so the simulator can be tuned from ideal-average behavior up to a more realistic reduced-order inverter model.

class src.control.svm_generator.InverterRealismConfig(enable_device_drop=False, device_drop_v=0.0, enable_dead_time=False, dead_time_fraction=0.0, enable_conduction_drop=False, conduction_resistance_ohm=0.0, enable_switching_loss=False, switching_frequency_hz=0.0, switching_loss_coeff_v_per_a_khz=0.0, enable_diode_freewheel=False, diode_drop_v=0.0, diode_resistance_ohm=0.0, enable_min_pulse=False, min_pulse_fraction=0.0, enable_bus_ripple=False, dc_link_capacitance_f=0.0, dc_link_source_resistance_ohm=0.0, dc_link_esr_ohm=0.0, enable_thermal_coupling=False, thermal_resistance_k_per_w=0.0, thermal_capacitance_j_per_k=1.0, ambient_temperature_c=25.0, temp_coeff_resistance_per_c=0.0, temp_coeff_drop_per_c=0.0, enable_phase_asymmetry=False, phase_voltage_scale_a=1.0, phase_voltage_scale_b=1.0, phase_voltage_scale_c=1.0, phase_drop_scale_a=1.0, phase_drop_scale_b=1.0, phase_drop_scale_c=1.0)[source]

Bases: object

Configuration container for all switchable inverter-realism blocks.

enable_device_drop: bool = False
device_drop_v: float = 0.0
enable_dead_time: bool = False
dead_time_fraction: float = 0.0
enable_conduction_drop: bool = False
conduction_resistance_ohm: float = 0.0
enable_switching_loss: bool = False
switching_frequency_hz: float = 0.0
switching_loss_coeff_v_per_a_khz: float = 0.0
enable_diode_freewheel: bool = False
diode_drop_v: float = 0.0
diode_resistance_ohm: float = 0.0
enable_min_pulse: bool = False
min_pulse_fraction: float = 0.0
enable_bus_ripple: bool = False
dc_link_capacitance_f: float = 0.0
dc_link_source_resistance_ohm: float = 0.0
dc_link_esr_ohm: float = 0.0
enable_thermal_coupling: bool = False
thermal_resistance_k_per_w: float = 0.0
thermal_capacitance_j_per_k: float = 1.0
ambient_temperature_c: float = 25.0
temp_coeff_resistance_per_c: float = 0.0
temp_coeff_drop_per_c: float = 0.0
enable_phase_asymmetry: bool = False
phase_voltage_scale_a: float = 1.0
phase_voltage_scale_b: float = 1.0
phase_voltage_scale_c: float = 1.0
phase_drop_scale_a: float = 1.0
phase_drop_scale_b: float = 1.0
phase_drop_scale_c: float = 1.0
__init__(enable_device_drop=False, device_drop_v=0.0, enable_dead_time=False, dead_time_fraction=0.0, enable_conduction_drop=False, conduction_resistance_ohm=0.0, enable_switching_loss=False, switching_frequency_hz=0.0, switching_loss_coeff_v_per_a_khz=0.0, enable_diode_freewheel=False, diode_drop_v=0.0, diode_resistance_ohm=0.0, enable_min_pulse=False, min_pulse_fraction=0.0, enable_bus_ripple=False, dc_link_capacitance_f=0.0, dc_link_source_resistance_ohm=0.0, dc_link_esr_ohm=0.0, enable_thermal_coupling=False, thermal_resistance_k_per_w=0.0, thermal_capacitance_j_per_k=1.0, ambient_temperature_c=25.0, temp_coeff_resistance_per_c=0.0, temp_coeff_drop_per_c=0.0, enable_phase_asymmetry=False, phase_voltage_scale_a=1.0, phase_voltage_scale_b=1.0, phase_voltage_scale_c=1.0, phase_drop_scale_a=1.0, phase_drop_scale_b=1.0, phase_drop_scale_c=1.0)
class src.control.svm_generator.SVMGenerator(dc_voltage=48.0, device_drop_v=0.0, dead_time_fraction=0.0, conduction_resistance_ohm=0.0, switching_frequency_hz=0.0, switching_loss_coeff_v_per_a_khz=0.0)[source]

Bases: object

Space Vector Modulation Generator.

The core SVM implementation remains an averaged inverter model. Realism is layered on top of that average model so users can independently enable or disable each effect.

__init__(dc_voltage=48.0, device_drop_v=0.0, dead_time_fraction=0.0, conduction_resistance_ohm=0.0, switching_frequency_hz=0.0, switching_loss_coeff_v_per_a_khz=0.0)[source]
set_sample_time(sample_time_s)[source]

Set simulation step used by bus and thermal state updates.

Return type:

None

reset_realism_state()[source]

Reset capacitor voltage, temperature, and cached telemetry.

Return type:

None

modulate(magnitude, angle)[source]

Generate 3-phase SVM voltages and apply enabled realism blocks.

Return type:

ndarray

get_maximum_voltage()[source]

Return presently available linear-range SVM voltage magnitude.

Return type:

float

property device_drop_v: float

Backward-compatible access to configured device drop.

property dead_time_fraction: float

Backward-compatible access to configured dead-time fraction.

property conduction_resistance_ohm: float

Backward-compatible access to conduction resistance.

property switching_frequency_hz: float

Backward-compatible access to switching frequency.

property switching_loss_coeff_v_per_a_khz: float

Backward-compatible access to switching-loss coefficient.

set_nonidealities(device_drop_v=0.0, dead_time_fraction=0.0, conduction_resistance_ohm=0.0, switching_frequency_hz=0.0, switching_loss_coeff_v_per_a_khz=0.0, enable_device_drop=None, enable_dead_time=None, enable_conduction_drop=None, enable_switching_loss=None, enable_diode_freewheel=False, diode_drop_v=0.0, diode_resistance_ohm=0.0, enable_min_pulse=False, min_pulse_fraction=0.0, enable_bus_ripple=False, dc_link_capacitance_f=0.0, dc_link_source_resistance_ohm=0.0, dc_link_esr_ohm=0.0, enable_thermal_coupling=False, thermal_resistance_k_per_w=0.0, thermal_capacitance_j_per_k=1.0, ambient_temperature_c=25.0, temp_coeff_resistance_per_c=0.0, temp_coeff_drop_per_c=0.0, enable_phase_asymmetry=False, phase_voltage_scale_a=1.0, phase_voltage_scale_b=1.0, phase_voltage_scale_c=1.0, phase_drop_scale_a=1.0, phase_drop_scale_b=1.0, phase_drop_scale_c=1.0)[source]

Configure inverter-realism parameters and explicit feature toggles.

Return type:

None

set_phase_currents(phase_currents)[source]

Provide phase currents used by current-dependent inverter effects.

Return type:

None

get_last_telemetry()[source]

Return telemetry from the last modulation step.

Return type:

Dict[str, float | int | bool]

get_realism_state()[source]

Return the complete inverter configuration and runtime state.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

set_dc_voltage(dc_voltage)[source]

Update the source DC bus value seen by the inverter model.

Return type:

None

class src.control.svm_generator.CartesianSVMGenerator(dc_voltage=48.0, device_drop_v=0.0, dead_time_fraction=0.0, conduction_resistance_ohm=0.0, switching_frequency_hz=0.0, switching_loss_coeff_v_per_a_khz=0.0)[source]

Bases: SVMGenerator

Extended SVM generator accepting Cartesian alpha-beta voltages.

modulate_cartesian(valpha, vbeta)[source]

Generate SVM voltages from Cartesian alpha-beta coordinates.

Return type:

ndarray

cartesian_to_threephase(valpha, vbeta)[source]

Convert Cartesian alpha-beta voltages to 3-phase quantities.

Return type:

ndarray

Coordinate Transformations for Motor Control

Provides Clark, Park, and Concordia transforms used in FOC algorithms. Both forward (3-phase to two-axis) and inverse transforms are supported.

author:

BLDC Control Team

version:

1.0.0

Added in version 1.0.0: Initial implementation of coordinate transforms

src.control.transforms.clarke_transform(va, vb, vc)[source]

Clarke transform (three-phase to stationary two-axis alpha-beta) assuming va + vb + vc = 0.

Parameters:

  • va (float) – Phase A voltage/current

  • vb (float) – Phase B voltage/current

  • vc (float) – Phase C voltage/current

Return type:

Tuple[float, float]

Returns:

(v_alpha, v_beta)

src.control.transforms.inverse_clarke(v_alpha, v_beta)[source]

Inverse Clarke transform (alpha-beta to three-phase)

Parameters:

  • v_alpha (float) – Alpha-axis quantity

  • v_beta (float) – Beta-axis quantity

Return type:

Tuple[float, float, float]

Returns:

(va, vb, vc)

src.control.transforms.park_transform(v_alpha, v_beta, theta)[source]

Park transform (alpha-beta to rotating d-q frame).

Parameters:

  • v_alpha (float) – Alpha-axis quantity

  • v_beta (float) – Beta-axis quantity

  • theta (float) – Rotor electrical angle [rad]

Return type:

Tuple[float, float]

Returns:

(v_d, v_q)

src.control.transforms.inverse_park(v_d, v_q, theta)[source]

Inverse Park transform (d-q to alpha-beta frame).

Parameters:

  • v_d (float) – d-axis quantity

  • v_q (float) – q-axis quantity

  • theta (float) – Rotor electrical angle [rad]

Return type:

Tuple[float, float]

Returns:

(v_alpha, v_beta)

src.control.transforms.concordia_transform(va, vb, vc)[source]

Concordia (also called symmetrical component) transform which is formally equivalent to Clark but keeps factor of 2/3 scaling. Useful when zero-sequence components are important.

Parameters:

  • va (float) – Phase A quantity

  • vb (float) – Phase B quantity

  • vc (float) – Phase C quantity

Return type:

Tuple[float, float]

Returns:

(alpha, beta) in same units

src.control.transforms.inverse_concordia(alpha, beta)[source]

Inverse Concordia transform (alpha-beta back to three-phase).

Parameters:

  • alpha (float) – alpha-axis quantity

  • beta (float) – beta-axis quantity

Return type:

Tuple[float, float, float]

Returns:

(va, vb, vc)

Previous Next
